title: Barons Hed
content:

{{Quote|The Empire sure knows how to ruin a perfectly good city.|[[Kyle Katarn]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''</ref>}}
'''Barons Hed''', also spelled '''Baron's Hed''' and sometimes called the '''"Fallen City"''', was once a great spaceport full of life, with busy market streets, entertainment and [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] that was located on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]].

==Geography==
A twenty [[kilometer/Legends|kilometer]]-long bridge with four lanes entered into the city. The bridge opened to a highway of six lanes and led to the [[farm/Legends|farmlands]] fourty kilometers away.<ref name="RA" />

The city was surrounded by an once-white wall, which however between [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]]<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Characters">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]<ref name="The Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> was allowed to turn into grey, even black at some points. The wall had a gate in the west side, and others at several directions. In the adjacent countryside herders would graze their [[gra]]s outside the gates.<ref name="RA" />

The city was built around the hill of [[Barons Knoll]]. The city was layered like a cake, roughly divided into [[High Town]], which marked the wealthiest residences, and [[Low Town]] for the poorest citizens. The [[Government House (Sulon)|Government House]] was built on top the Knoll. [[Rimmer's Alley]] was a street of the city. Barons Hed was filled with [[pyro flower]] gardens which however remained untended during the occupation, or repurposed as weapons emplacements and [[antenna/Legends|antenna]] farms.<ref name="RA" />

===Locations of interest===
;[[Club Hed]]: A music club in the city
;[[Barons Hed Lagoon]]: An artificial body of [[Water/Legends|water]] in the middle of a plaza, fed by a waterfall. The Lagoon was a large stone pool, and contained no vegetation or life outside of several [[Drugon]]s. Although it was located right next to an artificial river that flowed out of the city, it was not connected to it, being separated by a wall. Around the edges of the lagoon were a number of residences, a [[Barons Hed tavern|tavern]], and the [[House of Max]]. A bridge connected the side those buildings were on to the other side of the lagoon, which contained the entrance to an expansive bazaar.

==History==
The Sulonese built the city around the geographical feature of [[Barons Knoll]], which gave the city its name.<ref name="RA" />

When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] took control of the city, many businesses quickly went under. Locals remarked that "everything but the river dried up." Only a handful of die-hard businesses remained open, for the sole purpose of catering to the Imperial officers and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] that hung around. Outside the city limits, was a once bustling space refueling port, which saw very little business since the Imperials took over, depending on the ships of the Empire as customers, whenever one was close by.

Sometime before [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] several Sulonians who complained about Imperial policies, eventually publicly gathered in the city and protested in a non-violent demonstration. They managed to scramble before Imperial forces reacted, embarrassing the local Commandant who failed to arrest any; he resolved to the recorded [[Holovid/Legends|holovids]] (and the collaboration of a traitor) to identify and hunt them.<ref name="RA">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>

During the [[Subjugation of Sulon]] where the Imperials, who were under the command of the mysterious [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] and [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] and a certain captain [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], smashed the local [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] resistance, the Imperials "decorated" the city walls with the heads of the "traitors", along that of their leader, [[Morgan Katarn]], put on a stake.<ref name="Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref>

A huge administration tower, [[Government House (Sulon)|Government House]], was built in the center of the Knoll where the [[Governor/Legends|governors]] and law enforcers once resided. For a time it was home to the Jerec, who had built it during his governorship. The subjugation brought neglect to parts of the city, such as the walls and the gardens. Rebel attacks increased, and the once colorful gardens were repurposed to contain military equipment.<ref name="RA" /> 

This situation continued after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Jerec returned to Sulon while looking for the [[Valley of the Jedi]]. That time the city was full of troops, [[mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and [[bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] hired by the Empire. The gates were blocked by Imperial forces, residents were [[retinal scan/Legends|eye-scanned]] and others were interrogated in order to find Katarn, and lines formed outside the wall gates. The main reason was that Morgan Katarn's son, defector, Rebel [[agent/Legends|agent]] and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] novice [[Kyle Katarn]] was detected on the moon. Pairs of commandos on [[speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]], troops on [[Swift Assault 5|hoverscout]]s and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]]s regularly left the city for patrols.<ref name="RA" />

Katarn however managed to sneak into the city and fought his way through Barons Hed, which was garrisoned with troops and other thugs, and infiltrate the Palace.<ref name="RA" /><ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II" />

==Behind the scenes==
Barons Hed appears in [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II|''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'']] mainly as a playable map where the player can move and explore; an overview is seen in one of the cutscenes, where Kyle Katarn spies on it with his [[electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]].

In that game, level 5 is played in the town and levels 6 through 8 are played inside the administration tower. Originally, levels five and six were one complete level, but was split into two because of how big it was.<ref name="StratGuide">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>

One small section of the level 5 map is based directly on a previously unused [[Ralph McQuarrie]] concept painting known as '[[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] Commons'.

The player who chooses the [[Dark side of the Force|Dark Side]] path of the game, can attack and kill both enemies and the innocent bystanders living in the city.

Barons Hed appears again in ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', the novelisation of the game; more background and features are described in the narration, that are inconsistent to the in-game appearance; for example the canyon and the bridge leading into the city is nowhere to be seen when Kyle spies on the city, while the city itself doesn't seem to be built around a hill, although the Government House itself appears in the cutscenes and the game.
